rulings.Case Title: ERIC GUSTAFSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Peremptory Writ
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 5, 2025, line 2. PETITIONER ERIC GUSTAFSON'S Motion For Peremptory Writ. Petitioner's motion for writ of mandate is GRANTED. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DARIO ABRAMSKIEHN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 5, 2025, line 5. PLAINTIFF DARIO ABRAMSKIEHN'S Motion to Strike or Tax Costs. Plaintiff Dario Abramskiehn seeks an order striking or taxing defendant the City and County of San Francisco's costs. The motion is granted as to the City's expert witness fees ($41,956.07), the hearing reporter cost for the Debbie Esters deposition ($1100), and subpoena costs for Daren Billington and In-Depth Discovery ($555), and is otherwise denied. The total amount of costs taxed is $43,611.07, making the allowable cost award $18,522.45. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ARWELIE CABALLERO VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One [Motion 4 Of 4]
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 5, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF ARWELIE CABALLERO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part as stated below. Meet and confer efforts were adequate. The court orders the City to pay $250 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 30 days. The court orders some relief and the City's response to meet and confer efforts to cure deficiencies was unreasonably delayed. On the other hand. Plaintiff has achieved only limited success with this motion and his moving papers overreached. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ARWELIE CABALLERO VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - General, Set One [Motion 1 Of 4]
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 5, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF ARWELIE CABALLERO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories - General, Set One. The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part as stated below. Meet and confer efforts were adequate. The court orders the City to pay $250 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 30 days. The City's initial discovery responses were insufficient and it was slow to come into compliance with its obligations. But Plaintiff has achieved only limited success with this motion, his moving papers overreached, and his separate statement was unreasonably prolix in laying out concerns about responses the City had already amended. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ARWELIE CABALLERO VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Request For Admissions, Set One [Motion 3 Of 4]
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 5, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF ARWELIE CABALLERO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant To Request For Admissions, Set One. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to his requests for admission is denied as moot. Plaintiff agrees in his reply that the City's belated service of verifications moots the need for further relief. (Reply, pp. 7:28-8:1.) Plaintiff seeks sanctions. The court orders the City to pay $250 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 30 days. A motion for verifications is a matter of a page or two and Plaintiff's lengthy account of the deficiencies of superseded responses was unnecessary. On the other hand, the City's response to the deficiencies in its initial responses and the time it took to obtain a verification were unreasonably protracted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG MARBLE VS. SHERYL PATEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 5, 2025, line 9. PLAINTIFF CRAIG MARBLE'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. The court denies the motion to quash but orders counsel for defendant to submit to the first-look procedure. The deposition officer is directed to produce the material to plaintiff's counsel. Plaintiff's counsel will have two weeks from production to log any withheld records by provider, date, and sufficient description of the records to enable defendant to ascertain whether they relate to the condition or body part at issue. If plaintiff's counsel fails to provide the ordered log within this timeframe the deposition officer shall release subpoenaed records to defendant, who is ordered to hold them in confidence, to use them only in connection with this litigation, to disclose them only as necessary for this litigation, and not to disclose them to anyone who does not agree to be bound by these restrictions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
